title: Verilog FSM Traffic Light Controller
components: FPGA Development Board (e.g. Cyclone IV or Basys 3)
connections: Clock Input (50MHz) → FPGA Pin dedicated for Clock (e.g. Pin 23)
Red/Yellow/Green Outputs → FPGA GPIO Pins → Resistors → LEDs
tips: Always design FSM state changes to synchronize with the positive edge of the master clock to avoid timing glitches.
FPGA pins are susceptible to static discharge; place ESD protection diodes on exposed GPIO tracks.

title: Verilog UART Transmitter/Receiver
connections: FPGA Tx Output Pin → FTDI Rx Pin
FPGA Rx Input Pin → FTDI Tx Pin
tips: Generate baud rates by implementing a division counter relative to the input master clock.
Input signals (like Rx or buttons) should be synchronized using double-register buffers to prevent metastability.

title: Verilog SPI Master Controller
code:
/* Synthesizable SPI Master Controller Module */
module spi_master(
    input clk,
    input rst,
    input start,
    input [7:0] data_in,
    output reg sclk,
    output reg mosi,
    input miso,
    output reg ss_n,
    output reg [7:0] data_out,
    output reg done
);

  // SPI State Machine, clock polarity (CPOL), and phase (CPHA) setup
endmodule
tips: Design SPI output pins to latch data on one clock edge while sampling input lines on the opposite edge.
Verify the slave device's setup/hold timing constraints to adjust SCLK generation periods correctly.

title: Verilog 640x480 VGA Video Controller
connections: VGA Red/Green/Blue Pins → Resistor ladder networks (DAC) → Monitor RGB pins
tips: The 640x480 resolution requires a pixel clock of exactly 25.175MHz. Generate this by scaling the 50MHz clock down with a Phase-Locked Loop (PLL) or divider.
Implement a blanking interval buffer to ensure RGB color pins are outputting 0V during screen retrace intervals.

title: Verilog 4-Bit Digital Calculator
connections: DIP switches and push buttons connected to dedicated digital input pins
7-Segment segments and common anodes mapped to active output pins
tips: Verify division logic size. Hardware dividers utilize extensive lookup networks; constrain values to 4-bits to fit standard FPGA architectures.
Implement a binary-to-BCD conversion block (Double-Dabble algorithm) to render outputs accurately on two 7-segment displays.
